findings.description: The `callOpenAI` function does not handle HTTP response status codes, which could lead to unhandled exceptions.
The addition of the `?q=` query parameter in the GET /todos endpoint could expose the application to injection attacks if not properly sanitized.
The `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `GITHUB_TOKEN` are hardcoded in the `fix-agent.js` file, posing a risk of unauthorized access.

allFindings.description: The `callOpenAI` function does not handle HTTP response status codes, which could lead to unhandled exceptions.
The addition of the `?q=` query parameter in the GET /todos endpoint could expose the application to injection attacks if not properly sanitized.
The `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `GITHUB_TOKEN` are hardcoded in the `fix-agent.js` file, posing a risk of unauthorized access.
The `githubAPI` function resolves to an empty object if the response body is empty, which could lead to confusion.
The `writeFileContents` function does not handle potential errors when writing to the filesystem.
